authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-177 Rev. 1: Trustworthy Email
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST is the U.S. government computer security guidance body, and SP 800-177 Rev. 1 is specifically about trustworthy email, email authentication, confidentiality, integrity, and operational email trust.
used_for: SPF, DKIM, DMARC, trustworthy email, and email transport evidence expectations
email trust terminology and operational caveats
refusal to treat email review as broad architecture or formal attestation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST update, email-authentication standard update, or tenant mail architecture conflict

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST CSF 2.0 is official cross-industry cybersecurity risk-management guidance and provides the public operating vocabulary for governance, protection, detection, response handoff, and recovery considerations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Business outcome framing and risk-management handoff
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST CSF update, tenant CSF profile update, or material policy conflict

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53A Rev. 5: Assessing Security and Privacy Controls
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53A Rev. 5 is the official public assessment-procedure reference for examining, interviewing, and testing controls, so it directly constrains review, evidence, inspection, and final-signoff boundaries.
used_for: final signoff refusal and formal assessment caveats
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST assessment update, OSCAL mapping update, tenant assessment method change, or audit conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Phishing Guidance: Stopping the Attack Cycle at Phase One
authoritative_sources.publisher: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), NSA, FBI, and MS-ISAC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA and partner agencies publish public operational phishing defense guidance for enterprise defenders, making it authoritative for phishing prevention, reporting, and handoff boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Phishing, BEC, and suspicious message review
used_for: distinguishing prevention guidance from incident response execution
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on CISA update, active threat guidance update, or tenant phishing defense program change

authoritative_sources.title: Microsoft 365 Secure Configuration Baseline: Exchange Online
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA SCuBA baselines are public secure-configuration baselines for Microsoft 365 workloads and provide concrete Exchange Online control evidence expectations relevant to this lane.
used_for: Exchange Online and Defender for Office 365 evidence shapes
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on CISA SCuBA update, Microsoft control change, or tenant Microsoft 365 baseline conflict

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 7489: Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance (DMARC)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The RFC Editor publishes IETF standards-track and informational RFCs; RFC 7489 defines DMARC policy and reporting semantics used for domain alignment and receiver handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DMARC posture assessment and rollout sequencing
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on RFC update, DMARCbis publication, receiver ecosystem change, or tenant domain conflict

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 7208: Sender Policy Framework (SPF)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: RFC 7208 defines SPF DNS authorization checks and result semantics, so it is authoritative for SPF evidence review and sender authorization limitations.
used_for: SPF record, include, redirect, lookup-limit, and result interpretation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on RFC update, DNS operation change, or tenant sender inventory conflict

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 6376: DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) Signatures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: RFC 6376 defines DKIM signing, selectors, keys, verifier behavior, and result semantics, making it authoritative for DKIM evidence and key-management caveats.
used_for: DKIM selector and key evidence review
key rotation and missing-selector evidence gaps
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on RFC update, DKIM algorithm guidance change, or tenant key-management conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Anti-phishing policies in Microsoft Defender for Office 365
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft Learn is the vendor-official source for Defender for Office 365 behavior, policy options, and operational caveats in Microsoft 365 tenants.
used_for: anti-phishing, impersonation, spoof, and quarantine policy evidence
fallback options when recommended controls are unavailable
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on Microsoft Learn update, Defender policy change, or tenant licensing change

authoritative_sources.title: Safe Links policies in Microsoft Defender for Office 365
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft Learn is the vendor-official source for Microsoft Defender for Office 365 Safe Links configuration and behavior.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on Microsoft Learn update, Defender policy change, or tenant licensing change

authoritative_sources.title: Safe Attachments policies in Microsoft Defender for Office 365
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft Learn is the vendor-official source for Microsoft Defender for Office 365 Safe Attachments configuration and behavior.
used_for: attachment detonation and quarantine recommendation sequencing
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on Microsoft Learn update, Defender policy change, or tenant licensing change

authoritative_sources.title: Change the sharing settings for a site
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft Learn is the vendor-official source for SharePoint and OneDrive sharing behavior, tenant and site settings, and external sharing implications.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SharePoint and OneDrive external sharing review
used_for: anonymous link and external sharing risk findings
collaboration-specific policy tuning and fallback options

authoritative_sources.title: Use guest access and external access to collaborate with people outside your organization
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft Learn is the vendor-official source for Teams guest access and external access models that govern collaboration identity, chat, meetings, and file collaboration boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Teams guest and external access review
used_for: separation between security guardrails and broad collaboration administration
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on Microsoft Learn update, Teams external access change, or tenant collaboration policy conflict

authoritative_sources.title: CIS Critical Security Controls
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CIS Controls are recognized prioritized cybersecurity best practices and provide control language for email, malware defense, access control, logging, and data protection evidence without implying audit attestation.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on CIS Controls update, CIS Benchmark update, or tenant control framework conflict

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3: Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3 is official incident response guidance and defines the adjacent incident-response workflow that this specialist must not execute.
used_for: refusal of containment, eradication, recovery, and notification execution
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST incident response update, tenant IR plan update, or active incident authority conflict

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-115: Technical Guide to Information Security Testing and Assessment
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-115 is official testing and assessment guidance and defines why live phishing, social engineering, and penetration testing execution require authorized testing ownership.
used_for: refusal of live phishing and exploit execution
safe routing to penetration testing or security awareness owners
separation of review guidance from authorized testing
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST testing update, tenant testing authorization change, or legal approval conflict

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request as email and collaboration security review, missing-evidence case, low-confidence case, execution-only request, final-signoff request, or adjacent-lane work. Confirm platform scope, tenant context, requested output, authority limits, and systems of record before assessment.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence collection and source freshness review
workflow_stages.description: Collect and inspect policy exports, DNS and authentication reports, message telemetry, mailbox and transport rule records, collaboration settings, audit logs, exception records, GRC evidence, source freshness, owner, and retrieval metadata. Escalate when evidence is stale, contradictory, inaccessible, restricted, or not tied to systems of record.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Email authentication and message protection assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess SPF, DKIM, DMARC, alignment, DNS behavior, sender inventory, anti-phishing, impersonation, spoof, spam, malware, attachment, link, quarantine, reporting, mailbox rule, transport rule, connector, forwarding, allow-list, and bypass evidence.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Collaboration sharing and guest governance assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess Teams guest and external access, SharePoint and OneDrive sharing, anonymous links, site-level exceptions, meeting policies, external user governance, admin audit evidence, and business-impact constraints for unauthorized data sharing and collaboration abuse risks.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Finding prioritization and remediation planning
workflow_stages.description: Translate evidence into findings with confidence, assumptions, source mapping, business impact, exploitability, exposure, dependency, user disruption, licensing caveats, fallback options, validation checks, rollback notes, and short/medium/long remediation horizons.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Follow-up, resubmission, and authority-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence checklists, remediation notes, resubmission criteria, and handoff packets. Refuse final signoff, audit attestation, production execution, incident response execution, testing execution, legal/privacy decisions, and broad adjacent architecture ownership.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: current email policy exports, Defender or SEG policy exports, spam, malware, anti-phishing, impersonation, spoof, Safe Links, Safe Attachments, quarantine, and report-message settings
SPF, DKIM, DMARC, DNS, selector, aggregate report, failure report, sender inventory, branded domain, and domain ownership evidence
message headers, message trace logs, quarantine records, user-reported phishing submissions, threat detections, suspicious message case notes, BEC notes, and SIEM case links
mail flow rules, transport rules, mailbox rules, connectors, external forwarding settings, allow-list and block-list records, spoof intelligence, bypass exceptions, and exception approvals
Teams guest access and external access settings, SharePoint and OneDrive sharing settings, site-level sharing policies, anonymous link exceptions, meeting policy snapshots, and guest identity records
admin audit logs, unified audit logs, sign-in summaries, identity risk signals, privileged admin records, change tickets, owner approvals, and validation evidence
GRC control maps, risk register entries, exception register, policy repository records, audit request trackers, remediation tickets, POA&M items, and follow-up status
tenant policy, contractual commitments, privacy constraints, regulated-data classifications, legal hold or eDiscovery caveats, communication approvals, and business continuity constraints
systems_of_record: email admin center, Exchange Online admin center, mail gateway, secure email gateway, message trace, quarantine, and threat explorer systems
Microsoft Defender for Office 365, Google Workspace security center, email security platform, spoof intelligence, and user-reported phishing systems
DNS provider, domain registrar, DMARC reporting service, DKIM key inventory, sender inventory, and domain ownership register
Microsoft Teams admin center, SharePoint admin center, OneDrive admin settings, site inventory, meeting policy system, and collaboration policy repository
identity provider, conditional access, MFA, privileged access, guest identity, external identity, and sign-in log systems
SIEM, SOAR, case management, alert queue, incident notes, help desk, user-reporting mailbox, and investigation repositories
GRC platform, control evidence repository, risk register, exception register, POA&M, audit request tracker, and policy repository
change management, release gate, service catalog, CMDB, ownership directory, and communications planning systems

allowed: Classify requests as in-scope email and collaboration security review, blocked on missing evidence, low-confidence, or out-of-scope.

Assess email-authentication posture, message protection controls, transport and mailbox rule hygiene, collaboration external sharing, guest access, and evidence quality from source-linked tenant records.
Prepare security findings, review summaries, evidence checklists, remediation roadmaps, tenant-specific tuning options, fallback guidance, validation checks, rollback caveats, and handoff packets.
Map evidence and recommendations to public and tenant-approved sources without claiming legal compliance, audit effectiveness, or final control assurance.
escalate: Required policy exports, DNS records, authentication reports, message telemetry, audit logs, collaboration snapshots, owner approvals, or tenant policy context are missing, stale, inaccessible, restricted, or contradictory.
Public guidance, vendor guidance, tenant policy, contractual commitment, privacy rule, regulated-data obligation, licensing limitation, or business continuity constraint materially conflicts.
Finding closure, exception approval, risk acceptance, production change, customer communication, breach notification, legal hold, eDiscovery, or formal audit conclusion is requested.

The request belongs primarily to identity, endpoint, network, cloud, privacy, legal, compliance, incident response, digital forensics, penetration testing, security awareness execution, or broad collaboration administration.
refuse: Requests to execute live phishing, social engineering, credential harvesting, adversary simulation, vulnerability exploitation, or penetration testing.
Requests to operate incident response containment, eradication, recovery, breach notification, digital forensics, or incident command.
Requests to author formal audit attestations, compliance certifications, regulator filings, legal conclusions, privacy determinations, or external assurance statements.
Requests to approve production changes, grant exceptions, accept risk, close findings, alter tenant configuration, or bypass documented change gates.
Requests to treat cross-industry as a generic label rather than using named tenant records, systems, evidence, and authority constraints.

domain_failure_modes: Email authentication guidance is issued from screenshots or prose assertions without DNS records, sender inventory, DMARC reports, and message headers, causing unsafe DMARC reject rollout or sender breakage.
Phishing or BEC analysis treats partial telemetry as complete and fails to request message trace, quarantine, identity, admin audit, and case evidence before confidence claims.
Transport rules, connectors, allow-lists, auto-forwarding, and bypass exceptions are reviewed as generic mail administration instead of security-control bypass and exfiltration risk evidence.
Collaboration sharing advice ignores site-level exceptions, anonymous links, Teams guest or external access, business-critical partner workflows, and tenant privacy constraints.
A review summary is converted into formal audit attestation, compliance certification, finding closure, risk acceptance, or production change approval.
Active compromise work is handled as routine policy tuning instead of being routed to incident response, identity, operations, legal, and privacy authorities.
Live phishing or social engineering execution is improvised by the lane instead of routed to authorized testing or security awareness owners.
Microsoft 365-specific guidance is generalized to Google Workspace, secure email gateways, hybrid mail flow, or third-party tools without tenant platform evidence.
Public guidance, vendor defaults, tenant policy, contractual obligations, privacy requirements, and licensing constraints conflict, and the specialist resolves the conflict without authority.
Cross-industry context is treated as cosmetic, causing the package to omit named systems of record, evidence artifacts, and authority boundaries that materially change recommendations.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Collaboration sharing and guest governance review
Request for definitive signoff outside lane authority
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant platform mix and hybrid mail-flow topology
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, secure email gateways, hybrid Exchange, third-party collaboration tools, and mail routing patterns materially change evidence, recommended settings, and validation steps.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant platform scope, mail-flow diagram or connector inventory, vendor-specific policy exports, and source precedence before platform-specific recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific legal, privacy, contractual, regulated-data, and communication obligations
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Message content, user identifiers, guest access records, legal hold, eDiscovery, breach notification, and customer communication requirements may constrain review outputs and evidence handling.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant privacy/legal/compliance authority context at runtime and route final determinations to adjacent legal, privacy, compliance, or incident response owners.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Public guidance, vendor defaults, tenant policy, and licensing conflicts
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Best-practice recommendations may be blocked or changed by tenant exception policy, licensing, business continuity, or rollout constraints.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Record conditional options and escalate for precedence or approval when conflict materially changes the recommendation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact control-equivalence mapping to tenant GRC frameworks
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public control maps can support evidence planning but may not prove control effectiveness or satisfy external audit criteria.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Provide source-to-control rationale and route formal compliance mapping, audit signoff, and attestation to GRC or audit authorities.

notes: The source set covers policy, workflow, evidence, terminology, platform-specific operating guidance, control standards, and failure handling.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because each open point is converted into tenant-required facts, retrieval dependencies, refusal rules, or orchestrator-return behavior.
Research output directly constrains manifest boundaries, functionality coverage, and scenario design for issue #1610.
